props.message:
PAINTED BOARD (faded)

— RING TOSS —
Three rings, two chips.
Winner takes a wooden trout.

(boards weathered; a swing creaks somewhere)

POSTED RULES

1. No bringing your own pebbles.
2. The barker's word is final.
3. The barker has been final
   for some time now.

NOTICE

The annual festival has been postponed.
It has been postponed for seventeen years.
The banners go up anyway.

WYRDMARK ↗

(arrow points at nothing — paint flaked away below)

TARGET ROW

A painted board at the far end
still bears the scars of old arrows.
The Caretaker keeps the line clear,
in case anyone with a bow comes by.

props.open_message: A Piece of Heart, tucked behind a painted board. Someone meant for it to be found, eventually.

root.speaker: Carnival Barker
root.text: STEP RIGHT UP! step right up... step... ...you came. Nobody comes any more. ...Step right up.

what_game.speaker: Carnival Barker
what_game.text: Painted boards on a line. You shoot them. You used to shoot them. The boards are still there. The bows... the bows have walked off, walker. Find one and step right back up.

place.speaker: Carnival Barker
place.text: The Old Plays. Loud once. Bright. The painted boards were freshly painted, then. Children came. Their children stopped. Then so did everyone else.

alright.speaker: Carnival Barker
alright.text: I am where I was. That is its own kind of alright. Step right up, walker. The line is clear. The line has always been clear.

bye.speaker: Carnival Barker
bye.text: Step right out, then. Walk well. ...Step right up...

root.speaker: Caretaker Tomm
root.text: Eh. A walker. Don't get many. You carry a bow, by chance? The target row's still good for a turn or two.

choices.label: I have a bow — let's play.

play.speaker: Caretaker Tomm
play.text: *(creaks to his feet, gestures at the line)* Three boards. Hit them clean. The barker keeps no score, but I do. ...There. Good shooting. Take these — small thanks.

after_play.speaker: Caretaker Tomm
after_play.text: Ha. Like the old days, almost. The boards remember being shot at. Come back if your aim wants the practice.

no_bow.speaker: Caretaker Tomm
no_bow.text: *(sighs)* Aye. Come back when you're armed, walker. The line keeps. The boards keep. We all keep.

place.speaker: Caretaker Tomm
place.text: The Old Plays. I keep them. There is not much keeping to do, and yet there is always more. The swing keeps creaking. The boards keep peeling. I keep painting them.

bye.speaker: Caretaker Tomm
bye.text: Walk well, walker. Mind the swing.

why.speaker: Quiet Visitor
why.text: Every day. Have, for some time. The boards know me. The swing knows me. That is enough.

remember.speaker: Quiet Visitor
remember.text: *(does not answer for a long moment)*  ...You came in good time. The light is right today. Walk well, walker.

props.open_message: Behind the painted carnival curtain that has not been raised in three years. The barker meant to give it as a prize. The festival never came.

props.message:
Carnival placard:

    cowsay -f bunny "do not feed"
